        "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [Pitching an AI add-on to a current Data Engineer Academy student.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=22.0) ## Key Takeaways - [Peter pitched the 'DE in AI' add-on to Eze, a student finishing core Python modules, to accelerate his job search.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0) - [The add-on is distinct from a 'Generative AI' course; it focuses on using AI *within* data engineering to streamline pipelines.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0) - [A 50% launch discount ($3.5k vs. $7k retail) is available for the first 50 students, with financing options (e.g., ~$100/mo) offered to address Eze's financial constraints.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0) - [Eze will review the curriculum and decide by month-end, weighing the cost against the potential for a $200k+ job.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=617.0) ## Topics ### Eze's Background & Program Status - [**Background:** Full-stack → backend data engineer, now transitioning fully to data engineering.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0) - [**Program Status:** 1.75 months in; finishing core Python modules and will soon start end-to-end projects.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0) ### 'DE in AI' Add-on Pitch - [**Goal:** Use AI to streamline data engineering processes and accelerate the job search.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0) - [**Curriculum:**](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [LLM fundamentals & prompt engineering](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [Structured output validation](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [Embedded vector search & RAG](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [Pipeline orchestration for AI workloads](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [Data quality evaluation](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [Real-world projects](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0) - [**Rationale:** AI skills on a resume increase application responses by 20–30%, leading to more interviews and a faster job placement.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0) ### Pricing & Financing - [**Launch Offer:** $3,500 (50% off $7,000 retail) for the first 50 students.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0) - [**Eze's Concern:** The cost is 'steep' given existing financial commitments (mortgage, car, maxed-out credit cards from the Gold program).](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=438.0) - [**Proposed Solution:** Long-term financing plans (36, 48, or 84 months) to lower the monthly payment to a manageable level (e.g., ~$100/mo).](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0) ## Next Steps - [**Eze:** Review the curriculum and financing options.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0) - [**Eze:** Contact Peter by month-end with a decision.](https://fathom.video/share/3z8PyM1SkNNEUDtoejNztCyYst-KHpmF?tab=summary&timestamp=676.0)",

[**Skill Context:** Eze has strong Python algorithms experience but is now adapting to Pandas' declarative, SQL-like approach.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0) - [**Interview Focus:** Ryan confirmed that for data engineering roles, Pandas/DataFrames are the primary Python technical assessment, not complex algorithms.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0) ### Python FAANG Practice Plan - [**Goal:** Finalize the Python coding module by completing the FAANG-style Pandas questions.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=476.0) - [**Task:** Complete 21 questions total, with a specific breakdown:](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0) - [1 Beginner question per company](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0) - [1 Intermediate question per company](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0) - [1 Advanced question per company](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0) - [**Rationale:** This ensures exposure to the full range of question types and difficulty variations across different companies.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=542.0) - [**Deadline:** May 28, allowing for Eze's limited availability (~1 hr/day).](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0) ### AWS Cloud Module Plan - [**Context:** Eze has some AWS familiarity (e.g., S3, Athena, Glue) but needs deeper, hands-on experience with data migration and service integration.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0) - [**Prerequisites:**](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0) 1. [Create a personal AWS account.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0) 2. [Enable Cost Explorer to monitor usage and prevent unexpected charges.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0) - [**Tutorial Content:** The module covers core services (IAM, S3, Lambda) and less familiar ones (Kinesis, DynamoDB, ECS), with a focus on practical data migration between services.](https://fathom.video/share/NYSbL1s6Jwv2zxad8Tu64xHXm82jA9MC?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0) -         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [To align on Eze's Data Engineering upskilling plan and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0) ## Key Takeaways - [**SQL Focus:** The immediate priority is mastering advanced SQL for interviews. The sequence is: FAANG problems (subqueries) → SQL Mini Mock → DE Interview questions (window functions).](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=896.0) - [**AI Upskilling:** The AI Engineering module will be moved to the application phase. This strategy uses AI skills to boost interview rates (a reported 30–35% increase) and secure higher-paying roles before job searching.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2450.0) - [**Practice Strategy:** Prioritize consistency over perfection. Use a specific method for practice: solve independently, reference cheat sheets, then compare to the solution and re-solve from scratch to internalize concepts.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0) ## Topics ### Eze's Background & Goals - [**Background:** Hybrid Software/Data Engineer with ~3 years of experience.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0) - [**Goal:** Deepen expertise in Data Engineering to avoid being a 'jack-of-all-trades' and maximize career opportunities, including higher pay.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=176.0) - [**Motivation:** Proactive upskilling in response to the changing landscape driven by AI.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=163.0) ### SQL Upskilling Plan - [**Phase 1: FAANG Practice (Due April 9)**](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=896.0) - [**Task:** Complete 15 problems (5 beginner, 5 intermediate, 5 advanced) from one company.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0) - [**Focus:** Subqueries.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=896.0) - [**Optional:** Complete another 15 problems for more practice.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1042.0) - [**Phase 2: SQL Mini Mock**](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1151.0) - [**Task:** Book and complete a 30-minute mock interview (2 questions).](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1214.0) - [**Purpose:** Simulate a real interview and get feedback. Unlimited retakes are available.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1167.0) - [**Phase 3: DE Interview Questions (Due April 18)**](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1531.0) - [**Task:** After passing the Mini Mock, practice 15 questions (5 beginner, 5 intermediate, 5 advanced).](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1817.0) - [**Focus:** Window functions.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=901.0) - [**Daily Minimum:** 1 question from each difficulty level.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1681.0) - [**Phase 4: SQL Full Mock (TBD)**](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1214.0) - [**Task:** Book a 60-minute mock interview (3 questions) after completing the DE Interview questions.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1214.0) ### Practice Methodology & Resources - [**Practice Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0) 1. [Solve the problem independently.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0) 2. [Reference SQL cheat sheets for syntax.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2019.0) 3. [If stuck, compare your attempt to the solution to find differences.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=984.0) 4. [Delete the solution and re-solve the problem from scratch to internalize the concept.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0) - [**Syntax Management:** To avoid mixing syntax from Postgres, SQL Server, and MySQL, consistent practice is the solution. The more problems Eze solves, the faster he will adapt to the required syntax.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2182.0) ### AI Engineering Module Strategy - [**Initial Concern:** The AI module was scheduled too early, creating a risk of overwhelming Eze with too many topics at once.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2935.0) - [**Revised Strategy:** Move the module to the application phase.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2822.0) - [**Rationale:** This timing leverages AI skills to boost interview rates (a reported 30–35% increase) and secure higher-paying roles *before* Eze starts job searching.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2651.0) - [**Action:** Ryan will work with Kevin to reschedule the module in Eze's plan.](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=2935.0) ## Next Steps - [**Eze:**](https://fathom.video/share/4BpvsCzT4y8GjJaxtXA82xWXCp4AXyNa?tab=summary&timestamp=1125.0) -         "custom.cf_3JFRKeLpnUvmsOsVG478u5iAQZ5i5dPmvKbIkYLUaaH": "2026-04-02T23:30:00+00:00",
        "custom.cf_7EQ4NbImGdxDOQNwHVneg8JIjZj7wxprE3tMGhtwDOD": "NA",

        "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [Clarify program timeline, costs, and AI experience options.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=40.0) ## Key Takeaways - [**Timeline:** The 12-month agreement is a buffer; job placement is expected in ~7 months (3–5 training + 3 job search), not 12.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0) - [**AI Experience:** The optional $3,000 internship provides production-grade experience. The standard program offers guided projects, which Eze will frame using his company's AI context to create a strong resume narrative.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=524.0) - [**Costs:** The program fee covers all training and coaching for 12 months. Extensions for 1-on-1 coaching are paid (e.g., $100/mo for module access only).](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=433.0) - [**Payment:** The April 30 deadline is flexible. Eze will notify Kevin upon credit card arrival to prevent a finance flag and service disruption.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0) ## Topics ### Program Timeline & Structure - [**Initial Concern:** Eze was confused by the 12-month agreement, fearing it meant a 12-month path to a job.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0) - [**Clarification:** The 12-month term is a buffer. Based on Eze's strong SQL/Python skills, the expected path to placement is ~7 months:](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=234.0) - [**Training:** 3–5 months (10–15 hrs/wk)](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=324.0) - [**Job Search:** ~3 months](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=459.0) - [**AI Training:** The 47-module curriculum is not a prerequisite for job placement.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0) - [**Pre-Job Focus:** Learn AI basics and complete projects for interviews.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0) - [**Post-Job Focus:** Learn advanced modules on the job.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=375.0) ### AI Experience Options - [**Goal:** Gain AI project experience for the resume and interviews.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0) - [**Option 1: Standard Program (Included)**](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0) - [**Method:** Complete guided, self-paced projects.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0) - [**Resume Impact:** Cannot be listed as formal work experience.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0) - [**Eze's Strategy:** Frame projects using his company's AI context to create a compelling resume narrative.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=820.0) - [**Option 2: Internship (Optional Add-on)**](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=524.0) - [**Cost:** $3,000 one-time fee.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0) - [**Method:** Contribute to production-grade AI projects at a holding company.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=524.0) - [**Resume Impact:** Can be listed as formal work experience, which may accelerate job placement.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=728.0) ### Payment & Logistics - [**Payment Deadline:** April 30 is the target.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0) - [**Process:** Eze will notify Kevin upon credit card arrival → Kevin sends a payment link.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0) - [**Rationale:** This prevents a finance flag and service disruption that would occur if no payment is made by the end of the second week of April.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0) - [**Onboarding:** Eze's next meeting is with Ryan (April 6), a CSM and interview instructor.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0) ## Next Steps - [**Eze:**](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0) - [Notify Kevin upon credit card arrival to arrange payment.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0) - [Meet with Ryan (April 6) to discuss the plan and leverage available coaching.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0) - [**Kevin:**](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=936.0) - [Update the finance team on the payment timeline to prevent account flagging.](https://fathom.video/share/xne7K1pyHwN-JagsSvF1_hMBF2tHQMEE?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)",

Title: Database consultant-Mastery Job description: Key responsibilities include: Partner with Cybersecurity leaders, risk stakeholders, and non‐Cyber teams to define and deliver data‐driven Cyber use cases, aligned to enterprise risk priorities and frameworks (e.g., NIST CSF). Leverage scalable data pipelines, models, and architectures that enable Cyber analytics, AI, reporting, and advanced use cases across vulnerability management, threat exposure, control effectiveness, and risk insights. Work directly with data owners and platform teams to ingest, transform, normalize, and model security and IT datasets, ensuring data quality, lineage, and trust. Develop and operationalize analytics products including executive dashboards, strategic metrics, and operational reporting for leadership, governance forums, and front‐line Cyber teams. Prototype and productionize integrations across Cyber tools and enterprise data platforms, partnering closely with data engineering and architecture teams to ensure sustainability, performance, and supportability. Apply advanced analytics, data modeling, and automation techniques to translate raw Cyber telemetry into actionable outcomes, risk indicators, and decision support. Leverage AI‐assisted development and analytics workflows (e.g., Claude, code‐generation tools, AI‐augmented analysis) to accelerate engineering, insight generation, and experimentation—while operating within established security and data governance controls. Translate complex technical findings into clear, consumable narratives for executive and non‐technical stakeholders, connecting analytics outputs directly to Cyber risk, business impact, and outcomes. Serve as a thought partner and technical advisor, helping shape the Cyber data strategy, architecture direction, and future‐state analytics capabilities. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Data Engineering, Analytics, Information Security, or equivalent experience). 8+ years of experience working in Cybersecurity, risk, or technology domains, with deep hands‐on experience in data engineering, analytics, or data architecture. Demonstrated experience designing and building data pipelines, data models, and analytics architectures, including batch and/or streaming patterns. Practical experience partnering with or working on modern data platforms and tools such as Databricks, Redshift, Snowflake, Alteryx, or equivalent technologies. Working knowledge of Cybersecurity domains, including data privacy, data protection, and core security concepts (e.g., vulnerabilities, threats, controls, risk). Strong coding proficiency (e.g., Python, SQL) with the ability to assess multiple data sources and determine feasibility, data gaps, and engineering approaches to support Cyber use cases. Experience collaborating closely with data engineering, platform, and architecture teams to ensure long‐term operability and support. Preferred Qualifications Cybersecurity certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM) and/or data and analytics certifications (or equivalent advanced experience). Demonstrated experience applying advanced analytics techniques (e.g., predictive modeling, risk indicators, trend analysis) to Cybersecurity or technology risk problems. Experience incorporating or experimenting with AI‐enabled analytics or development tools (e.g., Claude, AI code assistants, agent‐based analytics) in a secure enterprise environment. Leadership experience as a technical lead, architect, or people manager within data, analytics, or Cyber practices. Strong proficiency with data visualization and BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) to deliver executive‐ready, actionable insights. Deep understanding of the Cyber data domain, including the ability to map analytics and outcomes to frameworks such as NIST CSF and familiarity with MITRE Telecommunication & CK. Experience working with enterprise data ecosystems, including data lakes, warehouses, and shared analytics platforms. Proven ability to operate as a consultative thought partner, shaping innovative use cases aligned to top Cyber risks and enterprise priorities. Track record of leading or delivering end‐to‐end data and analytics initiatives that materially improved Cyber risk management, visibility, or decision‐making. Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex technical outputs into business and risk context for senior leaders and non‐technical audiences.         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [Clarify SQL concepts for FAANG-style interview problems.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=9.0) ## Key Takeaways - [**`SUM` vs. `COUNT`:** Use `SUM` for totals (e.g., revenue), `COUNT` for row counts. This distinction is critical for correctly interpreting problem requirements.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0) - [**`COUNT(*)` vs. `COUNT(column)`:** `COUNT(*)` counts all rows. `COUNT(column)` counts non-null values in that column. They are interchangeable only if the column has no nulls.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0) - [**Problem-Solving Framework:**](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0) 1. [**Inspect Data:** Use `SELECT *` to understand table structure and nullability, which informs function choice.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0) 2. [**Deconstruct Problem:** Break requirements into logical SQL clauses (`SUM`, `GROUP BY`, `ORDER BY`, `LIMIT`).](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0) 3. [**Experiment:** Test alternative solutions (e.g., different `GROUP BY` columns) to build intuition.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0) ## Topics ### SQL Problem-Solving Framework - [**Initial Challenge:** Difficulty with beginner FAANG problems, specifically distinguishing `SUM` from `COUNT` and the nuances of `COUNT(*)` vs. `COUNT(column)`.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=9.0) - [**Core Distinction: `SUM` vs. `COUNT`**](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0) - [**`SUM`:** Aggregates *values* in a column to calculate a total.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0) - [**Use Case:** 'Total revenue,' 'total sales.'](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0) - [**`COUNT`:** Counts the number of *rows*.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=237.0) - [**Use Case:** 'Number of users,' 'number of orders.'](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=237.0) - [**Nuance: `COUNT(*)` vs. `COUNT(column)`**](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0) - [**`COUNT(*)`:** Counts all rows, including those with null values in other columns.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=913.0) - [**`COUNT(column)`:** Counts only rows where the specified `column` is *not null*.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=913.0) - [**Interchangeability:** They produce the same result *only if* the specified column has no nulls.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0) - [**Demonstration:** For the 'Microsoft Top Revenue Category' problem, the solution requires `SUM(revenue)` to find the total for each category, then `ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 3` to get the top three.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0) ### Applying the Framework: Spotify 'User Segmentation' Problem - [**Problem:** Classify users into segments (Power, Casual, New) based on listening activity.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=635.0) - [**Initial Code:** Used `COUNT(L.listen_id)` to count listens.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0) - [**Question:** Why did `COUNT(L.listen_id)` work, and what is the difference from `COUNT(*)`?](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0) - [**Analysis:**](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0) - [**Data Inspection:** `SELECT * FROM spotify_listens` revealed the `listen_id` column had no null values.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0) - [**Conclusion:** Because `listen_id` was non-null, `COUNT(L.listen_id)` and `COUNT(*)` were interchangeable for this specific problem.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0) ### Recommended Learning Resources - [**Live Classes:** Attend Beginner SQL classes, then progress to Intermediate and Advanced.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1012.0) - [**Office Hours:** Join group sessions (3–4 students) for collaborative problem-solving.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1032.0) - [**One-on-One Coaching:** Book sessions and post questions in advance for maximum productivity.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1157.0) - [**Slack Channel:** Post questions for quick answers or to prepare for coaching.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1134.0) ## Next Steps - [**Shineka:**](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1183.0) - [Revisit all beginner FAANG SQL problems, focusing on the `SUM` vs. `COUNT` distinction.](https://fathom.video/share/MwkLU72knULzx5h5b8ws7eaSUCfLEHE7?tab=summary&timestamp=1183.0) - [**Rationale:** Regular, short sessions are more effective for coding than sporadic, long ones.](https://fathom.video/share/x2i7LSEiZNiK4AfcUbNpuVTSgdaVhNxs?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0) ### Solution: New Study Plan & Practice Strategy - [**Commitment:** 1-hour daily morning session (Mon–Fri) to build a habit.](https://fathom.video/share/x2i7LSEiZNiK4AfcUbNpuVTSgdaVhNxs?tab=summary&timestamp=437.0) - [**Practice Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/x2i7LSEiZNiK4AfcUbNpuVTSgdaVhNxs?tab=summary&timestamp=1089.0) 1.         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "DEA <> HS Leadership Call - April 28 VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/1MhKhsCtzmQX_aozUxtjJZzm1Nh3ZAcF 32 mins (No highlights) --- Meeting Purpose Align on sales priorities and define a strategy to improve team performance. Key Takeaways - Top Priority: Improve cancellation rate, then close rate. This will increase ROAS, enabling higher ad spend beyond the current ~$300k/mo ceiling. - Setters Paid on Qualified Shows: A new $50/show incentive will be tied to a strict qualification standard (>$620 credit or $500/mo affordability), enforced by closers to ensure lead quality. - Leadership Development: Paddy will replace siloed 1-on-1s with joint setter/closer pod leader meetings to align on standards and foster a unified leadership culture. - Financing Upgrade: Paddy will negotiate a better deal with Clarity Pay (via contact Cameron) for prospects with 450+ credit, aiming to replace the low-payout (40%) Max Up program. Topics Problem: Ad Spend Ceiling & Unclear Performance Levers - Ad spend caps at ~$300k/mo due to diminishing returns, but the root cause is unclear. - Uncertainty: Is the ceiling caused by poor lead quality or low team performance? - Current Setter Metric (Activity): Insufficient. It drives volume, not quality, making it hard to diagnose issues. - Proposed Setter Metric: 'Closed sets' to directly measure the quality of appointments. Solution: Define & Enforce Lead Qualification - The team clarified the qualification standard to filter out low-value leads. - Standard: >$620 credit score OR ability to afford $500/mo. - Rationale: The affordability clause is critical. It validates leads with poor credit who can still pay, preventing them from being incorrectly disqualified. - Financing Context: - Climb (Max Up): For 620 credit → 90% payout. - New Incentive: Setters will earn $50 per qualified show. Closers will mark leads as qualified or not, creating an accountability loop. Solution: Upgrade Financing to Improve Payouts - A better financing option was identified that could replace the low-payout Max Up program. - Opportunity: Clarity Pay (via contact Cameron) offers a 70% payout for prospects with 450+ credit. - Action Plan: Paddy will negotiate a new deal, leveraging his new full-time role to secure a better rate. - Tactic: Negotiate as if DEA is a new client to get the best possible offer. - Constraint: The new option may have a 36-month term, which could make high-value deals (e.g., $30k) unaffordable. Solution: Restructure Leadership Meetings for Alignment - The new pod structure requires better communication between setter and closer leaders. - Problem: Siloed 1-on-1 meetings prevent leaders from aligning on performance standards. - Solution: Paddy will replace separate 1-on-1s with joint meetings for each pod's setter and closer leader. - Goal: Align leaders on standards, improve their pattern recognition for quality issues, and create a unified leadership culture. Next Steps - Paddy: - Negotiate a better financing deal with Cameron at Clarity Pay. - Implement joint setter/closer pod leader meetings. - Chris: - Finalize and distribute the Loom video explaining the new qualification criteria. - Create a Google Form for closers to mark lead qualification. - Decide on retaining Will for ongoing leadership development. - Will: - Confirm the financing provider and specific terms (credit score, payout) from the Fanbases contact.",

        "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "DEA <> HS Leadership Call - April 21 VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/rCf1rtCrPdt-JVVdTboTUQGa6fCMkkqC 29 mins (No highlights) --- Meeting Purpose Define and track quality sets for setter commissions. Key Takeaways - New Tracking for Setter Commissions: Closers will now mark sets as 'qualified' in a post-call form to enable setter commissions on shows, not just sales. - LeadFi Data Deactivated: Deactivating LeadFi data for Full Transitioners (FTs) saves $5k–$15k/mo, as the form already filters out 80–90% of bad credit risks. - Standardized Follow-up Cadence: Paddy will define a standard follow-up cadence, collaborating with Will to create a simple, rep-friendly system from Willow's complex automations. - AI Tooling Must Be Simple: Any AI tools for reps must be 'stupid simple' (e.g., smart lists) to prevent misuse and ensure adoption, with complexity hidden in the backend. Topics Tracking Quality Sets for Setter Commissions - Problem: How to commission setters on 'quality shows' (qualified leads who show up) when closers may not pitch unprepared leads, even if qualified. - Solution: Add a 'qualified set' field to the closer's post-call form. - Rationale: Closers can assess lead quality within the first 2–3 minutes of a call. - Definition: A qualified set meets criteria (e.g., >$70k income, >630 credit score) or expresses no price objection. - Risk Mitigation: - Closer Manipulation: Setters could incentivize closers to mark sets as qualified. - Counter: The financial upside for a closer from closing more deals far outweighs any potential setter incentive. - Pod Variation: The new data will reveal differences in offer rates between pods (e.g., Diana's pod may offer less), enabling targeted coaching. LeadFi Data & FT Prospects - Problem: High cost ($5k–$15k/mo) for LeadFi credit score data on Full Transitioners (FTs). - Decision: Deactivate LeadFi for FTs. - Rationale: The application form already identifies FTs, and 80–90% of those making <$70k have poor credit. The data confirms existing assumptions but doesn't change decisions. - Current Use: LeadFi is still used for Tier 1 and half of Tier 2 leads to assign top setters. Standardizing Follow-up Cadence - Problem: The follow-up process is inconsistent, with reps using varied, non-standardized cadences. - Action: Paddy will define a standardized, stage-based follow-up cadence. - Collaboration: Paddy will work with Will to translate Willow's complex automations into a simple, rep-friendly system. AI Tooling & Rep Adoption - Context: One rep uses a complex AI system (Claude, Manus, Notion) for tasks like drafting emails and creating hot lists, which has increased their sales. - Principle for Rollout: Any AI tool for reps must be 'stupid simple' to ensure adoption and prevent misuse. - Execution: Build complex logic in the backend (e.g., intent analysis) that surfaces as a simple interface for reps (e.g., smart lists). Team Headcount & Ratios - Problem: The closer-to-setter ratio is critical for ensuring closers' calendars are full, but headcount is hard to track due to churn and time off. - Current Count: ~32 closers, 41 setters. - Action: Chris will explore a system to track the ratio, potentially via Patricia's end-of-day report. Next Steps - Chris: - Add 'qualified set' field to the closer post-call form. - Create a shared collaboration doc for ongoing notes. - Share Paddy's KPIs and end-of-day reporting expectations. - Paddy: - Define a standardized, stage-based follow-up cadence. - Collaborate with Will to simplify follow-up automations for reps. - Paddy & Chris: - Monitor Diana's pod's offer rate to assess the 'offer less' strategy.",

          "html": "Achieved 1,700 sets and 83 closes in March but identified show rate of 45-55% (approximately 50%) as biggest concern, costing approximately $400,000 in lost revenue. Marketing achieved strong ROAS across channels with TikTok at 8.50, Google at 2.0, and Meta at 1.55, while testing lead magnets in April. Enhanced CSM onboarding extended to one hour with dedicated specialists achieving 100% five-star ratings; Job Apply team managing 121 clients with new referral partnerships. Implemented new development projects including AI Sales Agent, Student Engagement Dashboard, and content automation workflows while promoting tuition reimbursement as employee financing tool. March Sales Performance and Show Rate Concerns In March, the sales team achieved 1,700 sets and 83 closes, demonstrating strong performance in changing lives through education. The show rate has declined to approximately 45-55% (estimated at 50%), which is identified as the biggest current concern. The show rate issue accounts for approximately $400,000 in lost revenue due to lapses in communication and process execution. New marketing initiatives and team growth are contributing to current operational growing pains that are being actively addressed. Strategic Initiatives to Improve Show Rate Chris will create personalized VSL videos for different pathways (FTs, MTs, HDs, technical program managers, and AI) to strengthen setter-to-closer communication. Pod restructuring is planned to merge setter and closer pods into independent units, improving accountability and communication between teams. New setter scripts emphasizing connection, urgency, and closer edification are being implemented to increase show rates and close rates. Onboarding improvements for new closers and setters will be rolled out within 10 days, including best practices and CRM organization training. Marketing Performance and Lead Generation March saw the highest monthly ad spend across Google, Meta, and newly launched TikTok, with strong revenue returns from all channels. TikTok ads achieved a ROAS of 8.50 on $2,000 spend, significantly outperforming other channels. Meta generated $723,000 in revenue from $467,000 spend (ROAS 1.55), providing the highest volume of leads. Google achieved a ROAS of 2.0 from $163,000 spend, demonstrating the strongest return on ad spend ratio. A Facebook page outage disrupted lead quality early in the month, requiring approximately one to two weeks for the algorithm to recalibrate. Marketing will test lead magnets in April to evaluate if higher volume, lower quality leads improve overall ROAS. Content and Technical Development Updates The SME team completed an AI Agent TKI module that will be uploaded within one to two weeks. New SMEs Yosh (transitioned from CSM) and Tyler have joined the team on a full-time basis to support content creation. A Student Engagement Dashboard is being developed to help CSM and delivery teams identify struggling students and reduce refunds. An AI Sales Agent is in development to assist setters, closers, marketing, and other teams with ad copy writing. Content creation workflows are being automated to convert recorded AMAs and valuable sales calls into podcast-style videos using AI video generation. Job Apply Team Performance and Client Success The Job Apply team offboarded five clients in March, including three Silver clients with 15 interview plans resulting in multiple offers. Notable wins include Sanjay Rani (Diamond client) receiving an offer and Colton successfully negotiating from 90k to 100k plus a 10k bonus. The team received 14 client reviews and testimonials in March, with over 37-40 accumulated in Q1. Referral partnerships increased with 14 new partnerships established since February, showing consistent growth. The Ops team has grown significantly to 737 members with four pod leaders and 33 ops analysts, currently managing 121 clients. April focus areas include improving referral conversion rates, increasing offboarding to eight per month (from average of four), and expanding social media presence to LinkedIn and Reddit. Onboarding Improvements and Client Experience CSM onboarding has been revamped with dedicated onboarding specialists Afnan and Hector handling all new clients. Onboarding calls have been extended from 30 minutes to one hour, resulting in 100% five-star experience ratings in initial feedback. The enhanced onboarding process sets clear expectations regarding finances, job apply services, and program pathways. Strong initial onboarding experiences lead to better client satisfaction, positive reviews, referrals, and stronger accountability throughout the program. Trustpilot Review Verification Discussion Most current reviews on Trustpilot are not verified because students post reviews independently without being sent a specific verification link. Verified reviews require clients to receive an email with a specific link and complete the review through that link. The company has a few verified reviews but conversion rates may differ between verified and unverified review methods. Sales team has encountered objections from prospects regarding unverified testimonials and requests for verified reviews. Tuition Reimbursement as a Financing Tool Many larger companies offer tuition reimbursement programs, typically up to the federal tax limit (previously $5,250 annually). Tuition reimbursement programs allow employees to develop skills applicable to their current or target positions, paid back through paycheck. Anna Schenk successfully utilized this opportunity with a student whose company supported upskilling when the curriculum focused on professional development rather than job search. Companies offer tuition reimbursement as a retention and engagement tool, yet many employees do not take advantage of available benefits. The company can customize programming to align with student employer requirements to qualify for reimbursement eligibility. Stefan Lojanicic noted that previous attempts at tuition reimbursement were unsuccessful because the company is not a government-approved academy. Administrative Updates and Employee Engagement An unavailability form must be completed when team members are unavailable, ensuring safety and allowing proper staffing coverage. A staff questionnaire will be distributed to collect information about birthdays, work anniversaries, time zones, and other employee details. An hours and availability survey is being conducted to ensure proper pod pairing and adequate coverage across time zones and weekends. The LinkedIn lead generation program is available for team members interested in lending their LinkedIn accounts for company use with full privacy protection. A May step challenge is coming to encourage team health and fitness while working from home, with daily step targets. Pod Restructuring and Accountability Framework Setter and closer pods will be merged into independent units, each with a setter lead and closer lead operating as self-contained teams. Pod pairings will be determined by historical success weighting between specific setters and closers to maximize compatibility. Inbound lead distribution will be balanced across pods based on team availability and time zone coverage. The restructuring aims to create a meritocratic culture where high performers receive better opportunities and new team members are ramped faster. The new structure will include standardized SOPs and templates before implementation, with a full walkthrough of reasoning and procedures. Pod restructuring is expected to be rolled out within two weeks with detailed communication about structure, reasoning, and operational procedures. Closer Team Shout-Outs and Performance Metrics Kevin generated $196,000 in revenue, Abby achieved $168,000, and Peter K earned $117,000 in March. Enzo won the end-of-month dinner and drinks spiff and was encouraged to spend generously with at least a $250 bill. The closer team continues to face show rate challenges despite strong individual performance from top performers. Paddy Haynes emphasized the importance of radical candor and accountability between setters and closers in the reorganized structure. Setter Team Daily Goals Setters provided daily set targets ranging from two to five sets, with most aiming for three sets per day. Common challenges reported include low pickup rates, rescheduled appointments, and the need to balance quality prospects against volume. Matt Meoni guided setters on best practices including testing phone numbers for spam designation and maintaining consistent cycling of multiple numbers. The setter group emphasized commitment to hitting targets and improving script execution with newly trained frameworks. Company Culture and Team Recognition Team members shared personal photos from travels including Peru, Costa Rica, Barcelona, Yellowstone, Central America, and other locations. The 'DEA in the Wild' segment celebrated team member adventures and life milestones outside of work. Feedback Friday channel was promoted as a way to recognize team member accomplishments with extra recognition.         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "DEA <> HS Leadership Call - March 31 VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/g2D2GTTQs1mikZsazCA3ZxNduxtcc_XM 44 mins (No highlights) --- Meeting Purpose To address structural issues in the sales team and improve accountability. Key Takeaways - New Pod Structure: Pods will be 'married' (setter-closer pairs) to create tighter accountability loops. Leads will be distributed via a round-robin system to prevent setters from favoring top closers. - Leader Accountability: A one-page checklist will be created for pod leaders, defining success metrics like 20 role plays in the first 20 days (based on Logan's success) to standardize performance. - Leadership Reorg: Diana will be moved to manage Francis's pod (experienced reps) due to her emotional attachment to underperforming reps, which has cost the company ~$150k. Anna will take over Diana's pod (new reps) to enforce process adherence. - Marketing Spend: Ad spend will be tied to the number of 'ramped-up' closers, excluding new hires for their first month to prevent wasted spend on non-closing reps. Topics Problem: Lack of Accountability & Process Adherence - Financial Impact: An estimated $100k–$200k in cash collected is lost due to communication 'ball drops' between setters and closers. - Example: A setter failed to communicate an HT lead's profile to the closer, who then sent irrelevant pre-call materials, causing the lead to cancel a potential $30k–$50k deal. - Process Inconsistency: - Email Templates: 87 unique templates exist for a single purpose, despite the standard one having a 75%+ open rate and 90% response rate. - Script Adherence: New closers often modify scripts immediately, leading to poor results, while top performers like Logan follow the process and close deals quickly. - Leadership Inconsistency: - Time Commitment: Setter leaders dedicate significantly less time to their pods (0.5–2 hrs/day) than closer leaders (3+ hrs/day). - Process Adherence: Some leaders (Alejandro, Zahn) resist following established processes. Solution 1: New Pod Structure - Goal: Create tighter accountability loops within pods and enforce process adherence. - Structure: - Pods will be 'married' (a setter pod paired with a closer pod). - All lead communication and troubleshooting will be contained within the pod. - Lead Distribution: - A round-robin system will be used for lead allocation within each pod. - A general 'Top Closer' (TC) round-robin will serve as an overflow if a pod's internal calendar is full. - Rationale: This prevents setters from favoring specific closers and ensures an even distribution of leads. - Avatar Specialization: - The idea of avatar-specific pods (e.g., FT, MT, HT) was discussed but deferred. - Rationale: While FT leads have lower cash collected due to financing fees, the primary issue is accountability, not avatar-specific closing rates. Solution 2: Standardized Leader Checklist - Goal: Provide a clear, simple roadmap for pod leaders to define success and enable effective coaching. - Action: Matt will create a one-page checklist for pod leaders. - Key Metrics: - Role Plays: 20 role plays in the first 20 days (based on Logan's success). - Avatar Knowledge: Adherence to training on lead avatars. - Efficiency: Time to first dial, set, and sale. - Implementation: - The checklist will be a physical anchor for daily review. - Leaders will use it in 1-on-1s to track progress and provide targeted coaching. Solution 3: Leadership Reorganization - Problem: Diana's emotional attachment to underperforming reps violates SOPs and costs the company significant money. - Example: Keeping reps past performance deadlines has cost an estimated $150k. - Solution: Reorganize pod leadership to align skills with needs. - Diana: Moved to manage Francis's pod (experienced reps). - Rationale: Better fit for managing established reps who may be less receptive to strict process adherence. - Anna: Takes over Diana's pod (new reps) and Ali's pod. - Rationale: Anna's strength is strict process adherence, which is critical for ramping up new hires. Solution 4: Marketing Spend Adjustment - Problem: Ad spend was increased to fill calendars, but new, non-closing reps were receiving expensive leads, wasting marketing budget. - Solution: Tie ad spend to the number of 'ramped-up' closers. - New hires will not be included in the ad spend calculation for their first 30 days. - Rationale: This protects marketing budget by preventing spend on reps who are not yet converting leads. Next Steps - Paddy: - Present the financial impact of keeping underperforming reps to Diana. - Execute the leadership reorganization (move Diana to Francis's pod, Anna to Diana's and Ali's pods). - Matt: - Create the one-page leader checklist with key performance metrics. - Chris: - Update the closer tracking sheet with a 'ramped-up' status column. - Meet with Ali to address performance and potential negotiation. - All: - Finalize the new pod structure and lead distribution model after reviewing NARD data.",

[**Actionable Advice:** Adopt a five-step process: 1) Pseudocode, 2) Investigate data, 3) Verbalize thought process, 4) Validate approach with the interviewer, and 5) Maintain code clarity.](https://fathom.video/share/BVe3vT9iRKzhtxxEds6Kv5w9u6fgvz_i?tab=summary&timestamp=1963.0) ## Topics ### Mock Interview Performance - [**Beginner Question: 'Most Profitable Category (Microsoft)'**](https://fathom.video/share/BVe3vT9iRKzhtxxEds6Kv5w9u6fgvz_i?tab=summary&timestamp=342.0) - [**Task:** Find the product category with the highest total sales revenue.](https://fathom.video/share/BVe3vT9iRKzhtxxEds6Kv5w9u6fgvz_i?tab=summary&timestamp=342.0) - [**Intermediate Question: 'SuperCloud Customer (Microsoft)'**](https://fathom.video/share/BVe3vT9iRKzhtxxEds6Kv5w9u6fgvz_i?tab=summary&timestamp=947.0) - [**Task:** Identify customers who have purchased at least one product from *each* product category.](https://fathom.video/share/BVe3vT9iRKzhtxxEds6Kv5w9u6fgvz_i?tab=summary&timestamp=947.0) -         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [Onboard Sohel Rahman to the Data Engineer Academy program.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=9.0) ## Key Takeaways - [**Dual-Phase Strategy:** First, land a Senior/Lead SDET role using existing skills to secure income. Second, upskill to Data Engineering (DE) via a planned upgrade to the Gold tier.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=126.0) - [**Accelerated Onboarding:** The plan is condensed into one week to meet Sohel's goal of starting applications ASAP. This requires completing the first four steps before the first success coach meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1494.0) - [**Silver Tier Focus:** The current Silver tier provides job application support (resume, BQ prep, 15 interviews) but lacks the DE technical coaching needed for the long-term goal.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=716.0) - [**Assigned Success Coach:** Christian is the assigned success coach, with a first 15-min check-in scheduled for **Wednesday, April 30th at 11 AM EST**.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=3338.0) ## Topics ### Client Goals & Strategy - [**Background:** 25 years in SDET; current role: Software Engineer in Test VP.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=126.0) - [**Problem:** SDET career has hit a compensation ceiling.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=126.0) - [**Long-Term Goal:** Transition to Data Engineering for better long-term career strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=167.0) - [**Immediate Strategy:** Land a Senior/Lead SDET role to secure income before pursuing the DE transition.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=234.0) - [**Target Role:** Senior/Lead Software Engineer in Test (SDET).](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=287.0) - [**Target Salary:** $190k–$210k+ (based on prior $190k salary).](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1402.0) - [**Availability:** NYC (on-site, hybrid, or remote).](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1366.0) - [**Timeline:** Best case: 4 weeks to an offer. Realistic: 1–4 months, dependent on interview performance.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1460.0) ### Program Plan & Timeline - [**Urgency:** The plan is accelerated to meet Sohel's ASAP timeline.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1494.0) - [**Job Apply Team Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0) - [**Phase 1 (2–3 weeks):** Slow ramp-up to test the resume and gather market feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0) - [**Phase 2:** Scale to 200–300 applications/month → expected 2–6 interviews/month.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0) - [**Interview Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=917.0) - [The program provides 15 guaranteed interviews.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=917.0) - [Sohel's feedback after each interview is critical for identifying patterns (e.g., failing at a specific round).](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=929.0) - [If a pattern is found, applications will pause for targeted coaching to improve performance.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0) ### Silver vs. Gold Tier - [**Silver Tier (Current):**](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0) - [**Cost:** ~$11k (discounted from ~$15k).](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1030.0) - [**Focus:** Job application support for the immediate SDET role.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=287.0) - [**Includes:** Resume review, BQ prep, 15 interviews, negotiation support.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=830.0) - [**Excludes:** Unlimited 1-on-1 SME coaching.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=716.0) - [**Gold Tier (Future Goal):**](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=3493.0) - [**Cost:** ~$25k.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1105.0) - [**Focus:** Comprehensive Data Engineering upskilling.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=3493.0) - [**Includes:** Unlimited 1-on-1 SME coaching, bi-weekly mentorship, a personalized skill-gap plan.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=3493.0) - [**Path:** Sohel will use the Silver tier cost as a credit toward a Gold tier upgrade after landing a role.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=1142.0) ### Week 1 Onboarding Plan - [**Goal:** Complete steps 1–4 before the first success coach meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=3094.0) - [**Step 1: Support Video:** Watch the Skool support video and tag Hector on Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=2427.0) - [**Step 2: Resume Update:**](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=2494.0) - [Use the 'CEO template' to create a value-driven resume.](https://fathom.video/share/tot72S2JugxzbBv86PSzcCs69MVybNd3?tab=summary&timestamp=2494.0) -         "summary": {
          "html": "Negotiated Silver plan from $15k down to $11k with $500 minimum down payment and $100-200 monthly financing options. Outlined three-month software engineering job placement followed by optional data engineering track with credit rollover provisions. Silver plan includes skills assessment, resume optimization, application support by 15-20 person team, and 15 mock interviews. Scheduled one-week trial period starting Thursday at 1pm Eastern with onboarding manager Hector and follow-up meeting April 30th. Secured commitment for $500 down payment today with contract to be provided for review before trial period begins. Program Proposal and Pricing Structure Peter presented a phased approach combining software engineering job placement followed by data engineering training over 3-6 months. The Silver plan (retail $15k) was negotiated down to $11k, with a $500 minimum down payment. Gold plan costs $22k and includes data engineering education, one-on-one coaching, and end-to-end projects. Diamond plan is reserved for C-suite executives and VPs. The investment structure allows Sohail to pay approximately $100-200 monthly in financing rather than upfront. Credit rollover provisions allow Sohail to upgrade or apply unused funds toward additional services like AI training. Silver Plan Details and Services The Silver plan includes an initial skills assessment to evaluate Sohail's current software engineering capabilities. Resume review and ATS optimization to increase application response rates will be provided. A team of 15-20 people will handle job applications on Sohail's behalf. Sohail will receive 15 mock interview sessions to prepare for contract or full-time software engineering roles. Career services and job placement support are primary focuses of the Silver tier. Trial Period and Onboarding A one-week trial period was agreed upon to allow Sohail to familiarize himself with the team and process. The trial will include a behavioral mock interview and initial assessment. Onboarding call is scheduled for Thursday at 1pm Eastern Time. Trial period will run from Thursday through April 30th, with a follow-up meeting scheduled after seven days. Hector will manage all onboarding setup and channel configuration for the Silver plan. Contract and Next Steps Peter will send the Silver plan contract via email for Sohail's review, highlighting included deliverables. Contract will include specific language regarding upgrade options and credit rollover provisions at coach's discretion. Payment link for $500 down payment was sent via email to the address on file. Sohail committed to completing the $500 payment by end of business today. Peter will confirm receipt of payment and proceed with finalizing the onboarding process. Key Concerns Addressed Sohail raised concerns about landing a job while enrolled and wanting to pivot into data engineering mid-program. Peter clarified that credit rollover allows Sohail to apply unused funds toward new service tiers without losing value. The phased approach was designed to minimize cash flow burden given Sohail's current financial constraints. Peter emphasized that the $500 minimum down payment was the lowest amount he could negotiate with the finance team.",
          "text": "- Negotiated Silver plan from $15k down to $11k with $500 minimum down payment and $100-200 monthly financing options. - Outlined three-month software engineering job placement followed by optional data engineering track with credit rollover provisions. - Secured commitment for $500 down payment today with contract to be provided for review before trial period begins. ### Program Proposal and Pricing Structure - Peter presented a phased approach combining software engineering job placement followed by data engineering training over 3-6 months. - The Silver plan (retail $15k) was negotiated down to $11k, with a $500 minimum down payment. - Gold plan costs $22k and includes data engineering education, one-on-one coaching, and end-to-end projects. - Diamond plan is reserved for C-suite executives and VPs. - The investment structure allows Sohail to pay approximately $100-200 monthly in financing rather than upfront. - Credit rollover provisions allow Sohail to upgrade or apply unused funds toward additional services like AI training. ### Silver Plan Details and Services - A team of 15-20 people will handle job applications on Sohail's behalf. - Sohail will receive 15 mock interview sessions to prepare for contract or full-time software engineering roles. - Career services and job placement support are primary focuses of the Silver tier. ### Trial Period and Onboarding -         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [Mock recruiter interview for Christopher Walker.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=7.0) ## Key Takeaways - [**Problem:** Answers were too short (~30s), lacking the context, detail, and quantifiable results (numbers) needed to be compelling.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0) - [**Solution:** Adopt the STAR format (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to structure answers, aiming for a 1–2 minute length.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=568.0) - [**Action Items:** Watch a 'Tell Me About Yourself' video, rebook the mock interview, and attend a Gen AI course demo.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0) ## Topics ### Interview Performance Issues - [**Answer Length:** Answers were too brief (~30s), preventing a full demonstration of experience.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0) - [**Structure:** Lacked a clear narrative, making it hard to follow the logic and impact of the examples.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=568.0) - [**Quantification:** Results were vague ('it helped the company') and lacked specific metrics (e.g., money saved/earned, time reduced) to prove value.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0) - [**Delivery:** Rushed pacing and stuttering undermined confidence; poor camera framing created a perception of reading from a document.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=517.0) ### Coaching & Feedback - [**STAR Format:** Structure answers using STAR to provide a complete narrative.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=568.0) - [**Quantify Results:** Use numbers to prove impact (e.g., 'saved $X,' 'reduced time by Y%').](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=618.0) - [**Show Learning:** Conclude answers with lessons learned to demonstrate a proactive, growth-oriented mindset.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0) - [**'Tell Me About Yourself':** Frame the answer around past experience, current skills, and future goals that align with the target role and company mission.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0) - [**Delivery:** Maintain eye contact with the camera and use clear, confident pacing.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=716.0) ### Candidate Experience & Examples - [**Background:** 20+ years in software engineering and project management (Rite Aid, Citigroup, NCR, AIG).](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=41.0) - [**QA Integrity (AIG):** Refused to certify a project without full test case completion, escalating the issue to a senior program manager.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0) - [**Project Ownership (AIG):** Took ownership of a failing QA automation project, defining scope, timeline, and tasks to drive results.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0) - [**Complex Project (NCR):** Managed a tipping application migration from an on-site to a cloud solution, including daily data uploads.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0) - [**Project Management Process (NCR):**](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0) - [**Scheduling:** Used a detailed Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) to create a timeline with milestones.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0) - [**Risk Mitigation:** Identified a data mismatch risk in the RAID log, secured approval for reconciliation checkpoints, and led data cleanup efforts.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=311.0) - [**Agile Leadership (AIG):** Led cross-functional Scrum teams (engineers, QA, infra) for a QA automation project, using KPIs (velocity, cycle time) to track progress.](https://fathom.video/share/sTqNbzoT7pQ-ZXFNV2uTNn_6XtMSVvsb?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0) ## Next Steps -         "custom.cf_v8KO9e9P1dPh5b7gMyQCOuLhOlOCHjFdjmg4ZXwFYJB": "## Meeting Purpose [Align on the learning plan and define the coaching cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=142.0) ## Key Takeaways - [**Plan:** Use the live, updated Google Sheet as the source of truth; Rade will tag Stephen in Slack for access.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0) - [**AI Integration:** The 'Accelerated AI' content will be integrated by swapping 1–2 projects to maintain the program timeline.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0) - [**Support:** Rade confirmed a bi-weekly coaching cadence and highlighted his Mon–Thu 5–8 PM ET office hours for ad-hoc questions.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0) - [**Mock Interviews:** The Python mock interview process is now a round-robin system; Rade will provide the new scheduling link.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0) ## Topics ### Learning Plan & AI Integration - [Stephen needed a definitive learning plan, as his local spreadsheet was outdated.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=142.0) - [**Solution:** Use the live Google Sheet, which Rade will tag Stephen in via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0) - [**AI Integration:** The 'Accelerated AI' content will be integrated by swapping 1–2 projects from the existing plan.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0) - [**Rationale:** To avoid extending the program timeline.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0) - [**Document Repository:** The Google Sheet will also serve as a central repository for all related documents (e.g., resumes, mock interview notes).](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=351.0) ### Coaching & Support Cadence - [**Coaching:** Bi-weekly 1-on-1 sessions.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0) - [Stephen will schedule the next session for the week of June 8–12.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0) - [**Office Hours:** Mon–Thu, 5–8 PM ET.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0) - [**Purpose:** Ad-hoc questions; no scheduling needed.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0) - [**Communication:** Rade offered his phone number for direct calls/texts and is available for Slack huddles.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=520.0) ### Mock Interview Process - [**Problem:** The Python mock interview scheduling link in the old plan is outdated.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0) - [**Solution:** Rade will provide the new link for the round-robin system, which includes multiple interviewers like Tyler.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0) ## Next Steps - [**Rade:**](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0) - [Tag Stephen in Slack with the link to the live Google Sheet.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0) - [Send the new scheduling link for Python mock interviews.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0) - [**Stephen:**](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0) - [Schedule the next bi-weekly coaching session for the week of June 8–12.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0) - [Add his resume to the shared Google Sheet.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=351.0) - [Attend Python-focused meetings and Rade's office hours as needed.](https://fathom.video/share/Y3T1bqD8d1srQkq3nWq-NBzU2P-tXHrz?tab=summary&timestamp=884.0)",

[**Process:** Video call with screen share; no AI tools allowed.](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=557.0) - [**Success Rate:** Students rarely fail the second mock, showing the effectiveness of this feedback loop.](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0) ### SQL Path & Module Sequence - [**Initial Plan:** Follow the program's default sequence, which placed SQL after Python.](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0) - [**Adjustment:** Stephen's prior SQL experience (joins, subqueries) makes a full review unnecessary.](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0) - [**New Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0) - [**Focus:** Advanced SQL topics (e.g., window functions).](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=367.0) - [**Timing:** Practice advanced SQL during the Java Play stage, when the daily workload is lighter.](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0) ## Next Steps - [**Rade:** Send the Python mock interview scheduling link to Stephen via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0) - [**Stephen:**](https://fathom.video/share/Mn6QvAjVDsaJz7kk8REgV2ZzaWVTfaRu?tab=summary&timestamp=512.0) -
